title: Prediction of Complications in Lung Biopsies with Artificial Intelligence: A Preliminary Study and Early Findings
description_es: Este estudio preliminar investiga el uso de modelos de inteligencia artificial para predecir complicaciones en biopsias pulmonares, proporcionando hallazgos tempranos sobre la viabilidad de este enfoque.
abstract: Lung biopsy complications can have serious clinical consequences. This preliminary study explores the use of artificial intelligence models for predicting complications in lung biopsy procedures. Our early findings suggest that machine learning approaches can identify risk factors and patient characteristics associated with adverse outcomes, potentially enabling proactive intervention strategies.

citations.apa: Cribillés Pérez, M., Suárez Díaz, J. L., Maldonado Vicente, G., Martín-Rodríguez, J. L., & Herrera Triguero, F. (2026). Prediction of Complications in Lung Biopsies with Artificial Intelligence: A Preliminary Study and Early Findings. In 2026 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI).
citations.mla: Cribillés Pérez, M., J. L. Suárez Díaz, G. Maldonado Vicente, J. L. Martín-Rodríguez, and F. Herrera Triguero. "Prediction of Complications in Lung Biopsies with Artificial Intelligence: A Preliminary Study and Early Findings." 2026 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI), 2026.

contributions: Preliminary AI model for complication prediction
